metadata.name: Foreign Service Retirement System Rules
metadata.description: Comprehensive rules for all three Foreign Service retirement systems (FSRDS, FSRDS Offset, FSPS), including benefit formulas, eligibility requirements, COLA rules, survivor benefits, contribution rates, and annuity supplement provisions. Administered by the Department of State, not OPM.
sources: 3 FAM 6130 — Foreign Service Retirement Contributions and Deductions
3 FAM 6180 — Computation of Benefits Under FSRDS, FSRDS Offset, and FSPS
22 U.S.C. Chapter 52, Subchapter VIII — Foreign Service Retirement and Disability
Congressional Research Service R47084 — Federal Retirement Plans FAQ
NFC Appendix F (FSPS) and Appendix G (FSRDS) Computation Guides
notes: The Foreign Service retirement systems are the ONLY federal civilian DB pension systems administered outside OPM.
The Department of State's Office of Retirement (GTM/RET) adjudicates all Foreign Service retirement claims.
FSRDS is the Foreign Service equivalent of CSRS; FSPS is the equivalent of FERS — but with enhanced accrual rates.
All Foreign Service career members face mandatory retirement at age 65.
Virtual locality pay (Washington DC rate) is credited for overseas assignments in annuity calculations.
FSPS immediate retirees receive COLAs at ANY age — unlike regular FERS which delays COLAs to age 62.
As of FY2020: 33 active FSRDS employees, 15,208 active FSPS employees; 9,209 FSRDS annuitants, 7,220 FSPS annuitants.
covered_agencies: Foreign Service Corps — USDA (Foreign Agricultural Service)

administering_authority.retirement_claims: Bureau of Global Talent Management, Office of Retirement (GTM/RET), Department of State
administering_authority.financial_administration: Bureau of the Comptroller and Global Financial Services (CGFS/C), Department of State
administering_authority.fund: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund

retirement_systems.full_name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System
retirement_systems.applies_to: Foreign Service employees hired before January 1, 1984
retirement_systems.status: Closed to new entrants — 33 active employees remain (FY2020), ~9,209 annuitants
retirement_systems.pay_base: Highest average basic pay over any 36 consecutive months
high_3_includes: Virtual locality pay (Washington DC rate for overseas assignments, effective December 29, 2002)

cola_rule.description: Full CPI-W increase with no cap — same as CSRS
cola_rule.age_restriction: None — COLAs payable at any age for immediate annuitants

voluntary_immediate_unreduced.description: Age 50 with 20 years of creditable service
Any age with 25 years of creditable service

voluntary_deferred.description: Deferred annuity commencing at age 60 (or 50 with 20 years)

involuntary_immediate.description: Available upon involuntary separation with at least 5 years of creditable service

survivor_benefits.cost_to_retiree_pct: Varies — see Foreign Service Act Section 806

former_spouse_entitlement.description: Pro rata share of 55% of annuity based on length of marriage during creditable service

retirement_systems.full_name: Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System — Offset
retirement_systems.applies_to: Foreign Service employees with CSRS coverage who had a break in service after 1983 exceeding 1 year and had 5+ years civilian service as of January 1, 1987
retirement_systems.status: Closed to new entrants — small legacy population
retirement_systems.formula: Same as FSRDS, but annuity is reduced (offset) when eligible for Social Security
retirement_systems.pay_base: Highest average basic pay over any 36 consecutive months
retirement_systems.employee_contribution_note: 7.25% minus OASDI tax rate on income below SS wage base; full 7.25% on income above SS wage base

offset_rule.description: When annuitant becomes eligible for Social Security (typically age 62), the FSRDS portion of the annuity is reduced by the Social Security benefit attributable to Foreign Service employment
offset_rule.trigger: Eligibility for Social Security old age benefits (usually age 62)
offset_rule.note: The offset applies only to the FS service portion — not pre-FS or post-FS Social Security credits

cola_rule.description: Same as FSRDS — full CPI-W with no cap

retirement_systems.applies_to: Foreign Service employees hired on or after January 1, 1984
retirement_systems.analogous_to: FERS (Federal Employees Retirement System) — but with enhanced accrual rates
retirement_systems.tsp_matching_note: Same matching structure as FERS: 1% automatic + up to 4% match on employee contributions

formula.mra_plus_10_reduced: Standard formula reduced by 5% per year under age 62

retirement_systems.pay_base: Highest average basic pay over any 36 consecutive months
retirement_systems.max_annuity_cap: None — FSPS has no percentage cap (unlike FSRDS 70% cap)
retirement_systems.employee_contribution_note: 1.35% of basic pay to FSPS fund, plus OASDI taxes (6.2% up to SS wage base)
high_3_includes: Virtual locality pay (Washington DC rate for overseas assignments, effective December 29, 2002)
Law enforcement availability pay (for DSS special agents covered under 3 FAM 6150)

cola_rule.method: Tiered CPI — same formula as FERS
cola_rule.critical_difference_from_fers: FSPS immediate retirees (50/20 or involuntary) receive COLAs at ANY age. Regular FERS delays COLAs until age 62. This is a major advantage for Foreign Service employees who retire at 50.
cola_rule.mra_plus_10_exception: No COLA payable on MRA+10 annuity until age 62, unless annuity includes an FSRDS component

voluntary_immediate_unreduced.description: Age 50 with 20 years of creditable service — standard Foreign Service retirement
Any age with 25 years — but annuity computed at 1.0% per year (not 1.7%)
Age 62 with 5 years of creditable service — FERS-equivalent provision

voluntary_reduced.min_age: Minimum Retirement Age (same as FERS MRA table, based on birth year)
voluntary_reduced.description: Immediate annuity at MRA with 10+ years, but permanently reduced

voluntary_deferred.description: Annuity commences at age 62 (or 60 with 20 years of service)

involuntary_immediate.description: FSPS participants at class FS-02 or below, age 50+ with 20 years — immediate unreduced at 1.7%/1.0% formula

annuity_supplement.description: Monthly payment approximating the Social Security benefit earned during FS service, payable to FSPS retirees who retire before age 62 on an immediate unreduced annuity
annuity_supplement.computation: Calculated as if the retiree were a law enforcement officer retired under 5 U.S.C. 8412(d)
annuity_supplement.ends: Last day of the month in which the annuitant reaches age 62

earnings_test.description: Reduced by $1 for every $2 of earned income exceeding the Social Security earnings test limit
earnings_test.note: Applies to all earned income (government, private, contract, WAE). Does not count pensions or investment income.

former_spouse_entitlement.description: Pro rata share of 50% of annuity based on length of marriage during creditable service

survivor_benefits.max_combined_note: Combined total of all current and former spouse survivor annuities cannot exceed 50% of FSPS annuity

fsrds_to_fsps.description: FSRDS participants could elect FSPS during specific open windows (e.g., November 1, 1998 through April 30, 1999)
fsrds_to_fsps.frozen_service_rule: FSRDS service of 5+ years computed under FSRDS formula (2% per year, 70% cap); remaining FSPS service at 1.7%/1.0%
fsrds_to_fsps.frozen_service_under_5_years: Treated as FERS-to-FSPS transfer — all service computed under FSPS formula

csrs_to_fsrds.description: CSRS contributions can be transferred to FSRDS fund when employee enters Foreign Service

fers_to_fsps.description: FERS service is creditable under FSPS — combined service computed under FSPS formula

diplomatic_security_special_agents.description: Bureau of Diplomatic Security (DSS) special agents in the Foreign Service (2501 series) are covered under FSPS with the same 50/20 eligibility
diplomatic_security_special_agents.note: DSS agents in the Foreign Service use FSPS rules. DSS agents in Civil Service (1811 series) use FERS Special Category (6c) rules with mandatory retirement at 57.
diplomatic_security_special_agents.law_enforcement_availability_pay: Included in high-3 calculation for FSPS

physicians_comparability_allowance.description: A percentage of PCA is treated as basic pay for annuity computation for participants with 15+ years as a U.S. Government physician

virtual_locality_pay.description: Participants assigned overseas are credited the Washington DC locality pay rate (not their overseas basic pay) for retirement annuity calculation purposes
virtual_locality_pay.impact: Significantly increases high-3 for employees who serve most of their career overseas

key_differences.advantage: FSPS — significantly higher replacement rate for careers of 20 years

key_differences.fsps: Immediate for all immediate retirees regardless of age
key_differences.fers_standard: Delayed until age 62 for non-disability retirees
key_differences.advantage: FSPS — critical for Foreign Service officers retiring at 50

key_differences.fers_standard: MRA (56-57) with 30 years, or 60 with 20 years

key_differences.fsps: Available to all immediate retirees under 62
key_differences.fers_standard: Available to most immediate retirees under 62 (Special Retirement Supplement / SRS)
key_differences.advantage: Similar — but FSPS supplement computed as if LEO regardless of actual position
Context-dependent — FSPS rate is between FERS and FERS-RAE

twenty_year_replacement_rate_comparison.note: FSPS matches FERS LEO/FF enhanced formula exactly

population_statistics.source: FY2020 Actuarial Report of the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund

fsrds.note: Nearly extinct active population — virtually all FSRDS participants are now annuitants
